Example: A copy of your company's policy that supports hiring a diverse workforce, or similar documents.

An equal opportunity policy will confirm that your organisation is committed to treating everyone in the same manner and will not differentiate or put someone at a disadvantage because they are different. For example: Putting the best applicant into a job role who has earned that role or has the relevant experience, qualifications and knowledge of that job irrelevant of any differences between applicants.
It is acceptable to integrate your approach to diversity into your equal opportunity programme. 
Diversity is defined as employing people from a wide range  of backgrounds. Diversity includes:  race, sexual orientation, gender, ethnicity, disability, age, religion, culture, gender reassignment.
